overview: A midrange-control archetype that leverages Y'shtola's noncreature spell synergy to generate a resilient army of flying Spirits while using targeted removal and bounce to neutralize threats. It aims to out-value aggressive and combo decks through card advantage and board control, closing out games with a resilient token army or a late-game finisher.
win_conditions: Y'shtola herself with a board of flying Spirits, or a late-game spell like a board wipe + draw engine to reset and rebuild.
Finish with a combination of token pressure and targeted removal to clear the path.

keep_criteria: Keep hands with 2-3 lands (prioritizing duals/shocks if available), Sol Ring/Arcane Signet, and at least one early noncreature spell or removal.
Prioritize hands that can play a land + mana rock on turn 1, or land + noncreature spell on turn 2.
mulligan_criteria: Mulligan if you have 0-1 lands, no color fixers, or only creatures with no early interaction.

early_game: Turn 1: Play a basic land, then Sol Ring/Arcane Signet.
Turn 2: Play a dual land or fetch/shock, cast a cheap noncreature spell (e.g., bounce, counterspell, or draw) to trigger Y'shtola or build board presence.
If opening with a creature, play land, then creature, holding mana for interaction.
Sequence lands to ensure B/U/W availability by turn 3-4.
mid_game: Preserve mana for interaction until turn 4.
Use noncreature spells efficiently to trigger Y'shtola without overcommitting.
Manage graveyard by avoiding unnecessary recursion unless necessary.
Keep a hand of 3-4 cards to navigate disruption.
Commit threats only when you have removal or bounce ready for the opponent's response.
Use Y'shtola's -1/-1 ability to neutralize key threats without spending mana.
Hold back on casting expensive noncreature spells if the board is unstable.
late_game: Y'shtola herself with a board of flying Spirits, or a late-game spell like a board wipe + draw engine to reset and rebuild.
Finish with a combination of token pressure and targeted removal to clear the path.
Go all-in when you have card advantage, removal for their key threats, and enough mana to cast multiple noncreature spells in a turn.
Commit when the opponent is tapped out or has no answers to flying.

sequencing_tips: Y'shtola is vulnerable to board wipes and targeted removal
protect her or have a backup plan.
The deck thrives on noncreature spell synergy, so avoid overcommitting to creatures early.
Practice sequencing mana rocks and dual lands to avoid flooding.
Common mistake: casting too many noncreature spells without board presence, leaving you vulnerable to sweepers.
As a fringe archetype, expect unfamiliar hate cards
prioritize information gathering in the first few turns.

matchups.advice: Use cheap, multi-target, or instant-speed noncreature spells. Bounce or remove the counter source. Use Y'shtola's -1/-1 to neutralize blockers. Cast spells in response to their counterspells if possible.
Early interaction is key. Use bounce and cheap removal to slow them down. Bring in artifact destruction and counterspells. Don't let them stabilize; pressure early.
Prioritize cheap removal and bounce. Use Y'shtola's -1/-1 to neutralize key attackers. Hold up mana for interaction on opponent's turn. Stabilize with a board wipe if available.
Apply consistent pressure with noncreature spells and tokens. Force them to use resources on your threats. Use card draw to out-value them. Attack with flying Spirits to bypass ground blockers.

Win the resource war. Use hand disruption to limit their noncreature spells. Prioritize targeted removal over board wipes. Use Y'shtola's bounce to steal tempo. Control the board with -1/-1 and force them to overcommit.

common_threats: Watch for sweepers, counterspells, and targeted removal.
Note when opponents tap out for big threats or combo pieces.
Track graveyard usage for recursion decks.
Pay attention to mana development to predict their next play.
